mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects broad economic strength and growing corporate earnings potential, leading to positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2009 recovery has been characterized by steady, albeit slow, job growth.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows a positive correlation with S&P 500 20-day forward returns that is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past a critical threshold, it signals potential for aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: Instances where YoY CPIAUCSL is above 3.5% are not followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, a primary driver of the US economy. This drop precedes negative revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and leads to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal (3-month RSAFS growth < 0%) has a hit rate below 55% for predicting negative 20-day S&P 500 returns over the full backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment, a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a deflationary demand shock that the central bank is already fighting, implying poor forward returns as the shock propagates.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, the correlation between the 3-month change in UNRATE and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, which translates to higher corporate revenues and supports positive short-term equity market performance.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 1-month change in INDPRO and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) signals a strengthening housing market, which has a large positive multiplier effect on the broader economy through construction, materials, and wealth effects, driving equity prices higher.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market collapse was central to the 2008 crisis; its recovery is a key indicator.
failure_condition: Triggered periods do not show a statistically significant higher mean 60-day forward return compared to non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An inverted yield curve, proxied by the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) being significantly higher than inflation (CPIAUCSL), signals overly tight monetary policy that often precedes a recession and bear market.
evidence_refs: doc:Historical analysis of yield curve inversions preceding recessions.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits positive average 60-day returns in the 12 months following a trigger event across historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth is the ultimate driver of aggregate corporate earnings. Positive quarter-over-quarter growth signals an expanding economy, justifying higher equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic activity.
failure_condition: The sign of quarterly GDPC1 growth does not correctly predict the sign of the subsequent quarter's S&P 500 total return more than 55% of the time.
